name: Richard Gage
description: Richard Gage is an American architect and conspiracy theorist best known for his advocacy of the claim that the collapse of the World Trade Center towers (WTC 1, WTC 2, and WTC 7) on September 11, 2001, was a controlled demolition. Gage founded Architects & Engineers for 9/11 Truth (AE911Truth), an organization that promotes this theory. He argues that the official explanation of the collapses, which attributes them to fire-induced structural failure, is insufficient and that evidence points to the use of explosives. Gage's claims are based on observations of the speed and symmetry of the collapses, molten metal found in the debris, and alleged inconsistencies in the official reports. Mainstream scientific and engineering consensus, as represented by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) reports, strongly refutes the controlled demolition theory. NIST's investigations concluded that the collapses were caused by the impact damage from the planes and the subsequent fires, which weakened the steel structures of the buildings. Critics argue that Gage's interpretations of the evidence are selective and that he ignores or misrepresents the extensive data and analysis supporting the official explanation. The 9/11 Commission Report and NIST reports provide detailed accounts of the collapses based on extensive forensic evidence and modeling. While Gage's work has gained a following among some who question the official narrative of 9/11, his claims are widely rejected by experts in structural engineering, fire science, and explosives.
subtitle: Advocate of 9/11 controlled demolition theory

claims.claim: The WTC towers and WTC 7 were brought down by controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: Rapid and symmetrical collapse, molten metal in debris, squibs observed in videos, lack of jet fuel causing high temperatures.
claims.criticism: NIST reports attribute collapses to fire-induced structural failure after plane impacts. Molten metal explained by oxidation of steel and other materials. 'Squibs' are explained as dust and debris ejected during collapse. Collapse symmetry is a result of structural damage.

claims.claim: The official investigation by NIST is flawed and does not adequately explain the collapse of WTC 7.
claims.evidence: Freefall speed of WTC 7's collapse, lack of significant fire damage before collapse, NIST's admission that they did not model the entire collapse sequence.
claims.criticism: NIST thoroughly investigated WTC 7 collapse, identifying a critical structural failure due to fire-induced weakening. The 'freefall speed' claim is misleading; the collapse was close to freefall for a short period. NIST modeled the initiation of the collapse, focusing on the critical failure points.

claims.claim: Explosives, specifically thermite or thermate, were used to weaken the steel columns in the WTC towers and WTC 7.
claims.evidence: Presence of iron-rich microspheres in dust samples, evidence of unreacted thermite in debris, eyewitness accounts of explosions.
claims.criticism: Iron-rich microspheres are common in construction dust and not unique to explosives. No credible evidence of unreacted thermite has been found. Eyewitness accounts are often unreliable and subject to interpretation. Independent analysis of dust samples has not confirmed the presence of thermite residue.

keyFigures.name: Richard Gage
keyFigures.role: Founder of Architects & Engineers for 9/11 Truth, lead proponent

keyFigures.name: Jonathan Barnett
keyFigures.role: Structural engineer and critic of the controlled demolition theory

keyFigures.name: Zdeněk P. Bažant
keyFigures.role: Professor of Civil Engineering and Materials Science, Northwestern University. He published research that contradicts the controlled demolition theory.

David vs. Goliath (framing the conspiracy theory as a fight against powerful institutions)
